title: - What do you think, bhikkhus, which is greater: the few Simsapā leaves I have taken in my hand, or the leaves in the Simsapā…
content: - What do you think, bhikkhus, which is greater: the few Simsapā leaves I have taken in my hand, or the leaves in the Simsapā forest?" - "Venerable Sir, the few Simsapā leaves the Blessed One has taken in his hand are very few, and the leaves in the Simsapā forest are far more numerous." - "So too, bhikkhus, what I have directly known but not taught to you is far more numerous; what I have taught you is very little. But why, bhikkhus, have I not taught those things? Because they are not connected with the goal, not fundamental to the holy life, and do not lead to disenchantment, dispassion, cessation, peace, direct knowledge, enlightenment, Nibbāna. Therefore, I have not taught them. And what, bhikkhus, have I taught? 'This is suffering,' bhikkhus, I have taught. 'This is the origin of suffering,' I have taught. 'This is the cessation of suffering,' I have taught. 'This is the path leading to the cessation of suffering,' I have taught. And why, bhikkhus, have I taught these things? Because they are connected with the goal, fundamental to the holy life, and lead to disenchantment, dispassion, cessation, peace, direct knowledge, enlightenment, Nibbāna. Therefore, I have taught them.

title: There are these two things, bhikkhus, that are components of true knowledge. What two? Serenity and insight. When serenity is developed, bhikkhus, what benefit is…
content: There are these two things, bhikkhus, that are components of true knowledge. What two? Serenity and insight. When serenity is developed, bhikkhus, what benefit is to be expected? The mind is developed. When the mind is developed, what benefit is to be expected? Greed is abandoned. When insight is developed, bhikkhus, what benefit is to be expected? Wisdom is developed. When wisdom is developed, what benefit is to be expected? Ignorance is abandoned. Defiled by greed, bhikkhus, the mind is not liberated. Defiled by ignorance, wisdom is not developed. Therefore, through the removal of greed, there is liberation of mind; through the removal of ignorance, there is liberation by wisdom.

title: Master Gotama, just as among root fragrances, black sandalwood is considered supreme; among heartwood fragrances, red sandalwood is considered supreme; among flower fragrances, jasmine is…
content: Master Gotama, just as among root fragrances, black sandalwood is considered supreme; among heartwood fragrances, red sandalwood is considered supreme; among flower fragrances, jasmine is considered supreme. So too, the instruction of Master Gotama is considered supreme among instructions today.

title: Elder Nun Mother of Vaḍḍha: Vaḍḍha, never fall Into the realm of sensual desire, My son, do not share In continuous suffering. Happiness is for…
content:
Elder Nun Mother of Vaḍḍha:

Vaḍḍha, never fall
Into the realm of sensual desire,
My son, do not share
In continuous suffering.

Happiness is for the seer,
Without greed, doubt cut off,
Living cooled, subdued,
Without any taints.

That seer practices
The path to right view,
Ending suffering.
Vaḍḍha! Practice.

With confidence, the mother spoke
This goal for you.
Dear mother! I think that you
No longer have sensual desire.

Vaḍḍha! Whatever conduct — low, high, or middle —
Not even a little sensual desire is in me.

I have destroyed all taints,
Not heedless, meditating.
I have attained the three knowledges,
Done is the teaching.

Wonderful is my mother,
Encouraging, urging me,
With verses of truth,
Out of compassion.

I heard the mother's instruction,
The advice of my dear mother.
Moved, I practiced the Dhamma,
Attaining security from bondage.

I am energetic, diligent,
Day and night not lazy.
Well urged by my mother,
I attained supreme purity.

title: Thus, bhikkhus, if an elder bhikkhu speaks in the midst of the Saṅgha, the other bhikkhus say: 'Let the venerable ones be silent; the elder…
content: Thus, bhikkhus, if an elder bhikkhu speaks in the midst of the Saṅgha, the other bhikkhus say: 'Let the venerable ones be silent; the elder bhikkhu is speaking on the Dhamma and Discipline, and his words become a treasure to be stored away, like a person storing a Kāsi cloth in a fragrant box.' Therefore, bhikkhus, you should train yourselves thus: 'We will be like the Kāsi cloth example, not like the bark-cloth example.' Thus, bhikkhus, you should train yourselves.

title: Whoever transgresses one thing, Whoever speaks falsely, Whoever denies the world beyond, No evil remains undone by them. The miserly do not go to heaven,…
content:
Whoever transgresses one thing,
Whoever speaks falsely,
Whoever denies the world beyond,
No evil remains undone by them.

The miserly do not go to heaven,
Fools do not praise giving.
But the wise rejoice in giving,
And thereby become happy in the hereafter.

Better than ruling over the earth,
Better than going to heaven,
Better than lordship over the universe,
Is the fruit of stream-entry.

title: Then Māra approached the bhikkhunī Sisupacālā; having approached, he said to the bhikkhunī Sisupacālā: For whom are you shaven-headed, Appearing as a female samaṇa? Do…
content:
Then Māra approached the bhikkhunī Sisupacālā; having approached, he said to the bhikkhunī Sisupacālā:
For whom are you shaven-headed,
Appearing as a female samaṇa?
Do you not like false teachings?
Then for whom do you practice,
O foolish one?

( Sisupacālā ):
Those who follow wrong view,
Followers of outside sects,
I do not like their Dhamma,
I am not skilled in their Dhamma!
There is the Sakya clan.
From them the unequaled Buddha was born,
He has conquered all,
He has crushed Māra's army,
Everywhere, in all places,
No one can defeat him.
He is completely liberated,
Not bound by anything.
The all-seeing Dhamma-eye,
All kamma destroyed,
Liberated, attachments destroyed.
The Blessed One is my Teacher,
I delight in his teaching.

title: Therefore, Ānanda, I will teach you the Mirror of the Dhamma, so that a noble disciple, having realized this Dhamma, if he wishes, can declare…
content: Therefore, Ānanda, I will teach you the Mirror of the Dhamma, so that a noble disciple, having realized this Dhamma, if he wishes, can declare of himself: 'For me, there is no more hell, no more rebirth as an animal, as a ghost, no plane of misery, bad destination. I have become a stream-enterer, no longer subject to falling into the lower realms, assured of final enlightenment.'

title: Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu teaches the Dhamma to others extensively as he has heard and learned. He spends the whole day explaining the Dhamma to…
content:
Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu teaches the Dhamma to others extensively as he has heard and learned. He spends the whole day explaining the Dhamma to others, neglecting the peaceful life of seclusion, not striving to focus on internal serenity of mind. This, bhikkhus, is called a bhikkhu who is much in recitation but does not live in accordance with the Dhamma.
Furthermore, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu recites the Dhamma extensively as he has heard and learned. He spends the whole day reciting, neglecting the peaceful life of seclusion, not striving to focus on internal serenity of mind. This, bhikkhus, is called a bhikkhu who is much in recitation but does not live in accordance with the Dhamma.

title: Furthermore, bhikkhus, with sensual desire as cause, with sensual desire as condition, with sensual desire as reason, with sensual desire itself as cause, they take…
content: Furthermore, bhikkhus, with sensual desire as cause, with sensual desire as condition, with sensual desire as reason, with sensual desire itself as cause, they take up spears and shields, they don bows and arrows, they attack fortified citadels newly plastered with lime, and arrows fly, and swords flash. They are pierced by arrows, slashed by swords, drenched with boiling excrement, crushed with stones, and their heads are cut off. There they meet death or deadly pain. Bhikkhus, such is the danger of sensual desires, a mass of suffering visible here and now, with sensual desire as cause, with sensual desire as condition, with sensual desire as source, with sensual desire as reason.
